a:r.a:t: Strategy Materials Style Guide
Bridge Charts (1/2) | Title…
Preliminary
Cost Category
Reduction vs. US today
Rationale
Drivers
BuildCo
Policy
Labor
Productivity reaches 10 manhours per compensated gross ton
, surpassing global best-in-class yards (mid-teens in ROK)
Unlocked by fully robotic welding, automated block assembly, outfitting, and painting and coating enabled by developing greenfield site; Experience curve driven by USG-generated demand (SHIPS Act)
Raw Materials
Steel cost reduced ~10% (2035 values)
Achieved via contracted offtake w/ mills that warrant steel producer investment in mill efficiency, enabled by USG policy
Equipment
Procurement cost reduced ~35%
, in line with current Japan costs after including transport / OEM support fees (2035 values)
Achieved by purchasing equipment from Japan; SCF vessels not required to have US-manufactured parts
Overhead ex-D&A
Non-Production headcount reduced by 40-45%
; professional services, legal, and marketing spend cut by ~50-80%
Gain over US today also attributable to overhead spread across more hulls (25/yr) vs. Hanwha Philly (low single digits)
D&A
Impact of shipyard ITCs accounted for in Repricing at Target Margin line as Hanwha Philly’s D&A is not disclosed
n/a
Repricing at Target Margin
n/a
Targeting 5% EBIT margins
in line with US builders today
Vessel Investment Tax Credits
n/a
40% price reduction for vessel owners
Achieved by tax credit eligibility extension beyond ‘32
Newbuild Price Reduction Levers
($M, ’25E, for 3,600 TEU containership)
ITC eligibility requires flagging as US vessel for 10 years; without operating subsidies, vessels likely unprofitable vs. foreign-flagged given US crew, R&M, and dry-dock premiums
Note: (1) Raw material prices expected to see slower growth (~1% CAGR) than deflator 2.5%, driving differential between steel cost cuts and reduction vs. US Today | Source:
Building Ships in America
FRED (PPI, BE Inflation);

S&P (Current and forecast prices, FX rates, US trade volumes and destinations); Clarksons (Orderbook); Market participant feedback (Service life, build assumptions); FMI (Labor hours); Internal estimates
Incorporates reductions from prior page
Reduction found by subtracting 2035 costs (in real 2025 $) from 2025 costs for Labor, Raw Materials, and Equipment categories; same approach used on following pages
“US Today” represents Aloha-class vessels under construction at Hanwha Philly
JM: Arial 20 pt. font; default to template width for box (12.34”)
JM: Include Preliminary tag on all pages
JM: Include other tags on top as necessary
JM: Arial 10 pt font; bold title
JM: Arial 10 pt font; no bold for numbers
JM: Match category colors to chart bar colors
JM: Objects should generally be aligned with title Left and Right limits, but exceptions can be made for content-dense pages (this page is an example)
JM: Arial size 8-10 pt font for callouts
JM: Always include notes + sources, written as “Note” and “Source” even if there are multiple notes / sources; 8 pt Arial font
JM: Default text color is true black (#000000); true white (#FFFFFF) is also acceptable for dark filled cells)
JM: Always hyperlink sources when able
JM: Use a pipe ( | ) to reduce # of note / source lines
JM: Separate notes / sources with semi-colon
JM: Include currency, size (K, M, B), and real vs. nominal – for real, include year e.g., “’25E”; for nominal, state “nominal”)
